semanticCore: Palm tree, sifting, refining, or selection
quranicRelevance: Date palm trees (Nakhl/Nakhlah - 'wa jannatin min nakheelin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Date palm tree, or palm grove

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ (19:25): And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree (Mary's birth support).
discardedNoise: Sifting flour (نخل) using a sieve - original classical root.
summaryEn: The root refers to date palm trees, representing premium agricultural blessings, Mary's childbirth shelter, and structural metaphors.
summaryAr: تشير هذه المادة إلى النخل والتمور، ممثلة النعمة الزراعية الفاخرة للواحات، ومستند السيدة مريم عند مخاض الولادة.
rawText: اِنْنَخَلَهُ 8 He cleared it [or sifted it]; as also 🡻نَخَلَهُ and 🡻تَنَخَّلَهُ: (K [see سَفْسَفَهُ]:) or he took the best of it (S, Msb) to the utmost: (S:) or he chose the best of it for himself. (TA.) تَنَخَّلَ 5 see 8. نَخْلِىٌّ A cultivator of palm-trees: see عَطَلٌ. نَخڤلَ 1 see 8. نُخَالٌ Bran.
